title: Tetradenia stenophylla
intro: Tetradenia stenophylla, also known as African tea tree, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, characterized by its small, white flowers and essential oil-rich leaves. The leaves of Tetradenia stenophylla have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and skin irritations, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which provide relief from pain, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Tetradenia stenophylla include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Tetradenia stenophylla is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or essential oil, which are commonly used to treat respiratory issues, skin conditions, and digestive problems.
taxonomy: Tetradenia stenophylla is classified within the family Lamiaceae. This plant belongs to the genus Tetradenia, which is a part of the order Lamiales. Tetradenia stenophylla is further categorized under the subclass Asteridae. The family Lamiaceae is a group of flowering plants commonly known as mint family. The order Lamiales is a group of flowering plants that includes many species of herbs and shrubs.
